behaviors.reasoning: This behavior checks real model output quality (signature correctness from natural-language intent), not only deterministic local wiring.
This behavior validates contracts or state handoff across local modules; a pure unit test can miss cross-component wiring failures.
This behavior validates multi-step agentic adaptation across an iterative loop, where outcomes from earlier attempts influence later model behavior.
This behavior validates contracts or state handoff across local modules; a pure unit test can miss cross-component wiring failures.
This behavior requires chained agentic behavior across attempts (using prior failure context to change the next generation), not a single isolated model call.
This behavior validates orchestration and state handoff across local components (attempt control, result aggregation, structured error output).
This behavior explicitly targets non-agentic end-to-end wiring with deterministic stubbed model outputs across local components.
This behavior validates multi-step agentic orchestration where outputs from earlier steps affect later steps and final outcome.
